title: Care Corner Learning and Special Needs Support Programme
text:
Care Corner Learning and Special Needs Support Programme - SupportGoWhere
Care Corner Learning and Special Needs Support Programme
Share link
Provides literacy, language, and numeracy intervention to children aged between 5 and 13 years old (in mainstream schools) with learning difficulties.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Centre-based
Fees payable
Referral not required
SERVICE PROVIDERS
Find service providers near you
Collapse all sections
About this support

The programme provides learning support to children in mainstream schools struggling with learning difficulties such as:
* Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD)
* Autism Spectrum Disorder (ADD)
* Central Auditory Processing Disorder
* Developmental Delay
* Dyslexia
* Dyscalculia
* Dysgraphia
* Dyspraxia
* Specific Language Impairment
* Sensory Processing Disorder

Services includes:
* KidsReady (K1 to K2) - For children attending preschool / development supporting-learning support (DS-LS) programme who need more support in English, Mathematics, handwriting and fine motor skills
* KidsLearn (P1 to P6) - For primary school children needing learning support in English & Mathematics.

Who is this for?

Children aged 5 to 13 in mainstream schools struggling with learning difficulties

What to expect?

Care Corner will conduct a pre-intake assessment to screen if the child is suitable for the programme.

How to apply?

You may contact the service provider directly for enquiries or complete the online referral form: https://form.jotform.com/CC_LSN/lsnreferral

Information last updated on 11 Sep 2025
